title: Myrceugenia × bridgesii
taxonomy: Myrceugenia × bridgesii is a hybrid species within the family Myrtaceae. The Myrtaceae family consists of flowering plants commonly known as myrtles or eucalyptus. Myrceugenia × bridgesii is classified as a hybrid of the genus Myrceugenia, which comprises trees and shrubs native to South America. This hybrid species likely shares characteristics with its parent genera, such as its South American origin and evergreen foliage. The specific classification of Myrceugenia × bridgesii as a hybrid within the Myrtaceae family highlights its unique status within the plant kingdom.
uses: Myrceugenia × bridgesii, also known as the Chilean guava, has been used in traditional medicine due to its antioxidant properties.
